data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in this Android tombstone. The frames reference `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with Build ID `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`. 

To symbolicate these, I would need:
1. The debug symbols for `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with that specific Build ID
2. A tool like `llvm-symbolizer`, `addr2line`, or similar

The Build ID is `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`. This is the Mono runtime shared library from .NET MAUI.


I don't have direct access to the symbol files, but Microsoft publishes symbols for .NET runtime binaries on their symbol server. I can try constructing a URL to download the symbols using the Build ID format that Microsoft's symbol server expects, which follows the pattern of the filename, Build ID, and filename again. I'm trying to construct the correct URL format to fetch the debug symbols from Microsoft's server using the Build ID, but I'm realizing this might be a test file that doesn't have publicly available symbols, so I should first check what tools I have access to and whether the symbols actually exist online.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line objdump readelf 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "which dotnet 2>/dev/null && dotnet --version 2>/dev/null; which dotnet-symbol 2>/dev/null", "description": "Check for dotnet tools", "initial_wait": 10}

data.reasoningText:
Good, I have `addr2line` and `dotnet` available. Let me try to download the debug symbols for `libmonosgen-2.0.so` using the Build ID `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`.

Let me try the Microsoft symbol server. The typical approach is:

1. Install `dotnet-symbol` tool
2. Use it to download symbols

Or I can try to manually download from the symbol server.

Let me try installing dotnet-symbol first.

You can invoke the tool using the following command: dotnet-symbol
Tool 'dotnet-symbol' (version '9.0.661903') was successfully installed.
<exited with exit code 0>

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try to download debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server using Build ID
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
OUTDIR="/tmp/symbols_mono"
mkdir -p "$OUTDIR"

# Try Microsoft's debuginfod endpoint
curl -sf -o "$OUTDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  && echo "Downloaded from msdl (elf-buildid-sym)" \
  || echo "Failed elf-buildid-sym"

curl -sf -o "$OUTDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg2" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  && echo "Downloaded from msdl (_.debug)" \
  || echo "Failed _.debug"

# Also try the debuginfod format
curl -sf -o "$OUTDIR/debuginfo" \
  "https://debuginfod.elfutils.org/buildid/${BUILDID}/debuginfo" \
  && echo "Downloaded from elfutils debuginfod" \
  || echo "Failed elfutils debuginfod"
arguments.description: Download debug symbols from symbol servers

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download debug symbols from symbol servers
